title: Equanimity
justification: This talk provides the most comprehensive and structured treatment of equanimity, covering its definition, its role in the four illimitables (with near/far enemies), its place in the factors of enlightenment, its application in vipassana practice, and the four worldly conditions. It includes memorable anecdotes (the cat and mouse story, the Zen monk and the baby), practical guidance, and concludes with a guided meditation exercise—all elements that give it strong literary potential and a natural spine for a chapter.
summary: Bhante Bodhidhamma explores equanimity as the most important of all virtues, examining how it underpins the social virtues, balances the factors of enlightenment, and helps practitioners navigate the eight worldly conditions. The talk combines doctrinal depth with practical examples and concludes with a guided equanimity meditation.

relevance.note: Entirely focused on equanimity as foundation of virtues
